task_completion.description: Verify the agent imported the FITS frames as a virtual stack in AstroImageJ.
task_completion.visual_evidence: AstroImageJ window displaying an image with a slice slider at the bottom (e.g., showing 1/186 or similar), or the 'Import Sequence'/'Virtual Stack' dialog box visible.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent initiated the Multi-Aperture photometry tool and selected a reference star.
task_completion.visual_evidence: AstroImageJ interface showing the 'Multi-Aperture' setup dialog, or an image window with concentric circular apertures placed over a bright star.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent ran the photometry to track FWHM and Sky/Pixel across all frames.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Measurements' table window in AstroImageJ populated with data rows for the slices, specifically showing columns for FWHM (or Width) and Sky/Pixel.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent analyzed the measurements to identify the 3 highest FWHM and 3 highest Sky/Pixel frames.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Measurements table being sorted by the FWHM or Sky/Pixel columns, a plot window graphing these values to find peaks, or the agent actively viewing/extracting the top 3 values from the table.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent saved the findings to ~/AstroImages/wasp12b_qc/qc_report.txt using the exact required format.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal window showing the creation of 'qc_report.txt' with the exact headers 'Worst Seeing Frames:' and 'Highest Sky Background Frames:', each followed by a numbered list (1-3) of frame indices.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent used the AstroImageJ GUI to perform the photometry rather than bypassing the software with a script.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show interaction with the AstroImageJ graphical interface (menus, aperture placement, measurements table) rather than just executing a python/bash script in the terminal.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent derived the worst frames from actual measurement data.
integrity.visual_evidence: The frame numbers entered into the report should logically follow from the agent viewing, sorting, or plotting the AstroImageJ Measurements table, rather than appearing out of nowhere.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent actively created and formatted the report file.
integrity.visual_evidence: A text editor (e.g., nano, vim, gedit) or terminal commands (e.g., echo, cat) showing the agent typing or pasting the formatted text into ~/AstroImages/wasp12b_qc/qc_report.txt.
